title: An Anti-Join produces wrong result
description:
### What happens?

I perform an anti-join query on the following data, but it gets a wrong result.

```sql
CREATE TABLE t0(c0 VARCHAR);
CREATE TABLE t1(c0 VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO t1(c0) VALUES (NULL);
INSERT INTO t0(c0) VALUES (1);

select * FROM t1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM t0 WHERE null); -- correct, 1 row
select * FROM t1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM t0 WHERE ((t0.c0)!=(t1.c0))); -- wrong, no row
```
Due to the result of the first query, the second anti-join should produce 1 row instead of 0 row.  SQLite and MySQL can correctly execute it.

### To Reproduce

```
CREATE TABLE t0(c0 VARCHAR);
CREATE TABLE t1(c0 VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO t1(c0) VALUES (NULL);
INSERT INTO t0(c0) VALUES (1);

select * FROM t1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM t0 WHERE null); # correct, 1 row
select * FROM t1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM t0 WHERE ((t0.c0)!=(t1.c0))); # wrong, no row
```

### OS:

MacOS

### DuckDB Version:

v1.3.0-dev1736 c87ae7a200

### DuckDB Client:
